# Test cmd2mcpserver on real MCP binaries
#
# This test demonstrates analyzing actual binaries in the system

# Use mcpspy as an example since it's already built
# First, find mcpspy binary
exec which mcpspy
cp stdout mcpspy_path.txt

# Test cmd2mcpserver with verbose output on mcpspy
exec sh -c 'cmd2mcpserver -v -tool-def $(cat mcpspy_path.txt)'
stdout 'Binary Analysis:'
stdout 'Module: github.com/tmc/mcp/cmd/mcpspy'
stdout 'Command mcpspy records MCP interactions'

# Test the tool definition includes proper description
stdout '"description":\s*"Command mcpspy records MCP interactions"'
stdout '"name":\s*"mcpspy"'

# Test with mcp-probe
exec which mcp-probe
cp stdout probe_path.txt

exec sh -c 'cmd2mcpserver -v -tool-def $(cat probe_path.txt)'
stdout 'Binary Analysis:'
stdout 'Module: github.com/tmc/mcp/cmd/mcp-probe'

# Test on mcpdiff
exec which mcpdiff
cp stdout diff_path.txt

exec sh -c 'cmd2mcpserver -v -tool-def $(cat diff_path.txt)'
stdout 'Binary Analysis:'
stdout 'Module: github.com/tmc/mcp/cmd/mcpdiff'
stdout '"name":\s*"mcpdiff"'
